articles.title: Crew of Iranian ship seized by US forces evacuated to Pakistan, gov’t says
articles.summary: Twenty-two crew members from the seized Iranian ship MV Touska have been transferred to Pakistan for repatriation.

articles.title: Former NYC Mayor Giuliani hospitalised in ‘critical condition’
articles.summary: Spokesman says Giuliani in 'critical but stable' condition as President Trump hails 'true warrior'.

articles.title: Japan PM says Iran war oil crisis having ‘enormous impact’ in Asia Pacific
articles.summary: Sanae Takaichi makes comments during a visit to Australia, where she signs agreements on energy supplies.

articles.title: North Korean women’s club to play rare football match in the South
articles.summary: Naegohyang FC will play the South's Suwon FC on May 20 in the semifinal of the Women's Asian Champions League.

articles.title: All-round hero Hardie helps Babar Azam’s Peshawar Zalmi win PSL 2026
articles.summary: Hardie's 4 wickets and 56 runs help secure Peshawar's second PSL title with a five-wicket win over Hyderabad Kingsmen.

articles.title: How to escape Russia’s army: Soldiers serving in Ukraine seek a way out
articles.summary: Russia's army faces a desertion crisis as it continues to use waves of soldiers to attack Ukraine's defensive positions.

articles.title: Fireworks in Milan as Inter clinch Serie A title with a 2-0 win over Parma
articles.summary: Inter Milan win their 21st Scudetto, edging Napoli, with three matches remaining in the Italian football league season.

articles.title: Detained Gaza aid flotilla activists arrive in Netherlands
articles.summary: Two activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla arrived in the Netherlands after being released from Israeli custody.

articles.title: Trump says US to begin escorting ships in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has announced a naval mission to escort stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Oil prices flat as Trump’s plan for Strait of Hormuz fails to calm market
articles.summary: Brent crude is largely unmoved by Trump's announcement of the operation, dubbed 'Project Freedom'.

articles.title: Illegal ‘free party’ at French military site draws up to 40,000 ravers
articles.summary: Tens of thousands of partygoers gathered for an illegal “free party” at a military firing range near Bourges.

articles.title: Iran war live: Iranian military warns US of attacks over Hormuz mission
articles.summary: Iran protests as Trump announces 'Project Freedom' to free stranded ships out of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Thousands in India’s Manipur mark three years since ethnic clashes began
articles.summary: Thousands of protesters gathered in India’s Manipur state to mark three years since ethnic violence erupted.

articles.title: Three dead in suspected hantavirus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
articles.summary: Three of six passengers ill from a suspected rodent-transmitted virus have died, and one is in intensive care, WHO says.

articles.title: French left-wing’s Melenchon says he will run for president in 2027
articles.summary: Melenchon says he will make fourth bid for presidency with President Macron term-limited and Le Pen facing ban.

articles.title: Real Madrid stays alive in La Liga title race with victory at Espanyol
articles.summary: Two goals by Vinicius Jr allowed Madrid to remain in title contention with Barcelona ahead of El Clasico next Sunday.

articles.title: Iran lawmaker says Strait of Hormuz will not return to pre‑war state
articles.summary: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz will never return to the status quo that existed before the US-Israel war on Tehran.

articles.title: Trump says US will ‘help free up’ ships stuck in Hormuz Strait
articles.summary: US president says any interference with operation, which will start on Monday, will 'have to be dealt with forcefully'.

articles.title: Is Mali’s military government losing control?
articles.summary: A coalition of armed groups and rebels challenges the military government's authority.

articles.title: Kimi Antonelli wins Miami GP to extend F1 title lead
articles.summary: Nineteen-year-old becomes the first driver in F1 history to convert his first three pole positions into race victories.

articles.title: World Press Freedom Day marked in Gaza as journalist death toll rises
articles.summary: Palestinian journalists in Gaza marked World Press Freedom Day by honouring colleagues killed and targeted by Israel.

articles.title: Is Israel expanding into Gaza despite ceasefire?
articles.summary: Redi Tlhabi speaks to Ray Adams Row Farr from Forensic Architecture about Israel’s expansion into Gaza post-ceasefire.

articles.title: Germany’s Merz downplays rift with Washington despite US troop drawdown
articles.summary: German chancellor says criticism of Donald Trump's strategy in Iran unrelated to troop withdrawal announcement.

articles.title: Israel’s strategy of ‘permanent war’: A race against time?
articles.summary: Political analyst Daniel Levy says US policy is so ‘marinated’ in Israeli narratives, the two are indistinguishable.

articles.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian man in raid on Nablus in occupied West Ban
articles.summary: Israeli forces have raided the city of Nablus in the occupied West Bank, killing a 26-year-old man.
